name: Community Financial Services
description_short: Community Financial Services is a federally-insured credit union offering online banking, lending products, and mobile financial services to members.
description_long:
Community Financial Services is a member-owned credit union that provides comprehensive financial services to its members through both physical locations and digital channels. The organization operates as an NCUA-insured institution, meaning member deposits are protected by federal insurance up to standard limits. Based on their website presence and service offerings, they serve a regional membership base with a focus on accessible, technology-enabled banking.

The credit union offers a broad range of financial products including checking and savings accounts, online banking with bill pay functionality, mobile banking via the TouchBanking app with remote deposit capture, loan products (auto loans for new and used vehicles, HELOCs, credit cards with balance transfer options, and mortgages), and surcharge-free ATM access through the Allpoint network of 43,000 ATMs worldwide. Their current promotional rates include used auto loans as low as 5.50% APR, new auto loans at 5.00% APR, HELOCs at 7.25% APR, and Visa balance transfer cards at 5.9% APR. They also offer mortgage programs for both purchase and refinance scenarios.

What distinguishes Community Financial Services is their emphasis on digital-first banking accessibility combined with traditional credit union member benefits. They provide remote deposit capture through mobile banking, allowing members to deposit checks from anywhere using a smartphone. The availability of 43,000 surcharge-free ATMs through Allpoint partnership significantly extends their physical accessibility beyond their own branch network. Their online loan application system allows members to apply for loans on any device, any place, and any time, combining convenience with traditional lending services.

The credit union operates with standard limitations typical of financial institutions. While they offer competitive rates on some products, rates are subject to change without notice and are contingent on credit approval. Members must maintain certain account types (such as a checking account) to access specific features like bill pay. The website provides limited information about membership eligibility, account fees, minimum balance requirements, or specific terms and conditions, which would be important factors for prospective members to understand before joining.
services: Online banking with account balance checking and fund transfers across accounts
Bill pay service for online bill payments anytime from any device
Mobile banking app (TouchBanking) with full account access
Remote deposit capture via smartphone camera for check deposits
Surcharge-free ATM network access through Allpoint (43,000 ATMs worldwide)
New auto loans starting at 5.00% APR
Used auto loans starting at 5.50% APR
Home equity lines of credit (HELOC) at rates as low as 7.25% APR
Credit cards with balance transfer options at rates as low as 5.9% APR
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinance programs
E-statement delivery and digital account management
Lost or stolen card reporting (debit and credit card separate lines)
pros: Access to 43,000 surcharge-free ATMs worldwide through Allpoint network partnership
Remote deposit capture via mobile app allows check deposits from anywhere using smartphone
Competitive auto loan rates: new vehicles at 5.00% APR and used vehicles at 5.50% APR
Online banking with bill pay accessible on any device for flexible financial management
TouchBanking mobile app with full account access, transfers, and balance monitoring
Offers mortgage programs for both home purchase and refinance scenarios
NCUA-insured deposits provide federal protection for member accounts
cons: Limited membership eligibility information disclosed on website; unclear who can join
No transparent fee schedule or account minimum balance requirements published online
Rates subject to change without notice and all lending is contingent on credit approval with potential restrictions
Mobile app requires prior online banking enrollment; creates barrier for new digital-only users
Website provides minimal detail on loan terms, conditions, or application timelines
best_for: Credit union members seeking competitive auto and home lending rates with digital banking flexibility
Individuals who value surcharge-free ATM access and remote deposit capabilities via mobile phone
Members interested in balance transfer credit cards to consolidate high credit card debt
Borrowers needing HELOC options for home equity access at reasonable rates
diagnosis: Community Financial Services is best for existing or prospective credit union members seeking a full-service financial institution with competitive lending rates, strong digital banking capabilities, and broad ATM access. The main caveat is that membership eligibility criteria and account requirements are not disclosed on their website, and potential members should contact the credit union directly at 908-245-1650 to understand their specific membership qualifications and fee structure.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Community Financial Services — NCUA-insured credit union in ROSELLE, NJ. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
